title: System topology (multi-app ODC architecture)
description: One document describing how a SYSTEM decomposes into apps + libraries across the ODC 3-Layer Canvas. The architect pass PROPOSES this (from a flat domain spec); harness-arch-gate (architecture.py) DISPOSES — a monolith fails the 6 no-monolith invariants. Each app here expands into its own app_spec.v0 document for the per-app build. See ARCHITECTURE_DECOMPOSITION.md.

layer.description: ODC 3-Layer Canvas layer. Only 'core' may own persistent entities.

kind.description: ODC asset kind (the app_create kind). Must be consistent with the layer.

context.description: Bounded context this app owns (data-owning apps only). Exactly one; a context maps 1:1 to one Core app.

actor.description: For enduser apps: the persona/role this app serves.

owns.description: Names of persistent entities this app OWNS. Only 'core' apps may be non-empty.

exposes.description: The app's public contract other apps may consume.

consumes.description: Cross-app dependencies. Each entry names a producer app and the elements consumed from it (entities are READ-ONLY; writes go via serviceActions).

foreignKeys.description: Declared FK edges from this app's local entities. Every target MUST be an entity this app OWNS (OS-DPL-50205: a local entity cannot FK a cross-app entity).

from.description: the local entity holding the FK

target.description: the referenced entity — must be owned by this app

raisesEvents.description: Events this app RAISES. enduser apps must not raise events (orchestration concern).

process.description: For orchestration BusinessProcess apps: the BPT process definition (mirrors app_spec.processes).

activities.description: Convenience: BPT activities, if declared inline. Presence forces layer=orchestration.
